The available operation modes are:
• BatteryLife (default setting):
This algorithm is self-adapting and aims to maximise the battery's lifespan. For a detailed explanation of its functionality, please
refer to the BatteryLife [8] chapter for a description of its functionality.
• Conventional algorithm 1:
12V system: OFF when Vbatt < 11.1V, ON when Vbatt > 13.1V.
24V system: OFF when Vbatt < 22.2V, ON when Vbatt > 26.2V.
48V system: OFF when Vbatt < 44.4V, ON when Vbatt > 52.4V.
• Conventional algorithm 2:
12V system: OFF when Vbatt < 11.8V, ON when Vbatt > 14.0V.
24V system: OFF when Vbatt < 23.6V, ON when Vbatt > 28.0V.
48V system: OFF when Vbatt < 47.4V, ON when Vbatt > 56.0V.
• Always off:
The load output is always OFF.
• Always on:
The load output is always ON.
• User defined algorithm 1:
OFF when Vbatt < Vlow.
ON when Vbatt > Vhigh.
• User defined algorithm 2:
OFF when Vbatt < Vlow or Vbatt > Vhigh.
ON when Vbatt is between Vlow and Vhigh.
• Automatic Energy Selector (AES):
ON when Vbat > Vhigh.
OFF when Vbat < Vhigh for the pre-selected AES time.
OFF when Vbat < Vlow.

8.12. Error code overview
The error codes in the following subchapters are potentially displayed in the VictronConnect app, on a remote display or a
connected GX device. For the most up-to-date error overview, see this link: https://www.victronenergy.com/live/mppt-error-codes.
Furthermore, the solar charger utilises specific LED indications to signal particular errors. For an overview of these LED codes,
please refer to the Victron Toolkit app.
8.12.1. Error 1 - Battery temperature too high
This error will auto-reset after the battery temperature has dropped. The solar charger will stop charging to prevent damaging the
battery. The battery temperature can be received by an external sensor (like Smart Battery Sense or BMV) or measured by the
charger when this feature is available.
8.12.2. Error 2 - Battery voltage too high
This error will auto-reset after the battery voltage has dropped. This error can be due to other charging equipment connected to
the battery or a fault in the solar charger.
This error can also occur if the battery voltage (12, 24, 48V) is set to a lower voltage than the connected battery.
8.12.3. Error 17 - Solar charger overheated despite reduced output current

• Error 38: First, disconnect the solar panels and then disconnect the battery. Wait for 3 minutes, then first reconnect the battery
and then the panels.

(normally Equalisation or Absorption voltages). It can also take a minute to reset the fault.

8.12.11. Error 40 - PV Input failed to shutdown
If the solar charger is unable to turn off the PV input, it will go into a safe mode in order to protect the battery from over-charging
or having a high voltage on the battery terminals. In order to do that, the solar charger will stop charging and disconnect its own
output. The solar charger will become faulty.

When these errors show, the PV Input is internally shorted in order to protect the battery from over-charging.

If a ground current is detected in the system during normal operation, take the following steps:
• First, thoroughly inspect all the equipment connected to the system and check for any ground faults.

output.

Float voltage
This setting sets the float voltage.
Re-bulk voltage offset
This setting sets the re-bulk voltage offset. This offset voltage is used to determine when a charge stage stops and the bulk stage
starts again, i.e. the charge cycle resets and starts at the first charge stage again.
The re-bulk voltage is calculated by adding the re-bulk voltage offset to the lowest voltage setting (normally this is the float stage).
An example: If the re-bulk offset is set at 0.1V and the float voltage at 13.8V, the charge cycle will restart once the battery voltage
drops below 13.7V (13.8 minus 0.1) for one minute.
Equalization voltage
This setting set the equalization voltage.
Equalization current percentage
This setting sets the percentage of the "maximum charge current" setting that will be used to calculate the equalization charge
current.

The solar charger has a built-in internal temperature sensor.

The PV voltage should always stay within the maximum rated limit of the solar charger, as indicated in its product name, type
plate and Technical specifications [62]. The solar charger can sustain damage based on the extent of the PV voltage height, and
it's important to note that such damage is not covered by warranty.
Should the PV voltage exceed the maximum rated PV voltage, the solar charger will cease charging, showing an overvoltage
error #33 with rapid blinking of the absorption and float LEDs. Charging resumes only when the PV voltage drops 5V below the
rated maximum voltage.
During investigations into high voltage issues, it's essential to review the VictronConnect app, solar charger display, or GX device
history. Check for the highest PV voltage recorded each day (Vmax) and past overvoltage warnings.

3. Features
3.1. Automatic battery voltage detection
The solar charger automatically detects supported (e.g 12V, or 24V) system voltage (battery voltage) on first power up. If a
different system voltage is required at a later stage, or if the solar charger is connected to a 36V system, this can be manually
configured in the solar charger settings.
3.2. Outstanding MPPT algorithm
Ultra fast MPP tracking
The solar charger contains an ultra fast MPPT controller. This is especially beneficial when the solar light intensity is constantly
changing, as is the case during cloudy weather. Because of the ultra fast MPPT controller, 30% more energy is harvested
compared to solar chargers with a PWM controller and up to 10% more compared to slower MPPT controllers.
Optimal solar yield
The solar charger has an innovative tracking algorithm. It will always maximize energy harvest by locking to the optimum MPP
(Maximum Power Point). If partial shading occurs, two or more maximum power points may be present on the power-voltage
curve. Conventional MPPTs tend to lock to a local MPP, which may not be the optimum MPP.

3.7. VE.Direct port
The VE.Direct port is used to communicate with the solar charger. It can be used for several purposes:
• To connect to a monitoring device, such as a GX device or the GlobalLink.
• To connect with the VictronConnect app.
• For external control.
• To program the load output behaviour.
Special cables or interfaces are needed to connect to this port:
• VE.Direct cable - used to connect to a GX device or the GlobalLink.
• VE.Direct to USB interface - used to connect via USB to the VictronConnect app.
• VE.Direct Bluetooth Smart dongle - used to connect via Bluetooth to the VictronConnect app.
• VE.Direct TX digital output cable - used for streetlight control or to create a virtual load output.
• VE.Direct non inverting remote on/off cable - used to remotely switch the solar charger on or off.
3.8. Load output
The solar charger is equipped with a physical and virtual load output.
3.8.1. Physical load output

disconnects the loads if the battery voltage drops too low, safeguarding the battery against too-deep discharges.

assembled_user_prompt:
You extract an interaction profile for one piece of marine equipment from excerpts
of its official manual(s). You record observable facts about how humans interact
with the device. You do NOT judge importance or guide tier. You do NOT write guest
prose.

Rules:

1. Use ONLY the manual text provided. No general knowledge about this product or
   brand. Unmentioned = empty array / false / "unclear". Never guess.
   device.category_freeform must be the manual's own product category words
   (e.g. "charger/inverter", "MPPT solar charger") — never registry/taxonomy
   tokens such as electrical_dc, propulsion, freshwater_system.

2. Empty is meaningful. A busbar has no control surfaces and no operator actions;
   emit empty arrays.

3. audience per action, by the manual's own framing:
   - routine steps addressed to "you" → operator
   - "consult your dealer", "qualified personnel", installation-chapter procedures,
     DIP-switch / DIP table configuration → installer_or_technician
     (DIP configuration is always installer_or_technician + commissioning)
   - genuinely both → either

4. context per action: daily / situational / emergency / commissioning /
   maintenance. One procedure fitting two contexts → two entries.

5. Split actions: each action string is one verb phrase with one context.
   "monitor and configure via the app" → two actions (e.g. monitor → daily;
   configure → situational or commissioning per the manual's framing).
   Splitting bundled actions must never remove actions. Every distinct
   procedure the manual addresses to the operator must appear, including
   shutdown, restart, and fault/error-code procedures.
   has_emergency_procedure: true when the manual has an error-code / fault
   table or protective-disconnect recovery — not merely because an orderly
   service/storage shutdown exists (see calibration J).

6. control_surfaces on the base product vs. accessories sold separately: mark the
   latter optional_accessory: true. If a capability requires another product, also
   add a requires_devices entry with needed_for pointing at that field path
   (e.g. "control_surfaces[0]" or "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network").
   Never set needed_for to "" / null. Never attach requires_devices to a surface
   with optional_accessory: false except software_app (downloadable app for a
   built-in surface — record it with requirement_kind: software_app).
   Multiple requires_devices entries that share the same needed_for are OR
   alternatives (any one present on the vessel activates the capability);
   emit separate entries per alternative product — never stuff "A or B" into
   one description_verbatim when both are first-class options.
   requirement_kind (required on each entry):
     device — vessel line-item product (GX device, remote panel, safety relay)
     cable_or_consumable — cable/cord/dongle/consumable (VE.Direct cable)
     software_app — downloadable app (VictronConnect app)
     commissioning_tool — installer config/commissioning software
       (MasterAdjust, CZone Configuration Tool)

7. networks: record names verbatim in speaks (with physical_or_wireless when known).
   Ports/protocols that exist on the device belong in speaks even when their
   usefulness depends on another product — express that dependency with
   requires_devices, not by omitting the speak or zeroing the capability.
   Record a bridge ONLY if the manual explicitly describes translating/gatewaying
   between two named networks. Being connectable to a network is speaks, not bridges.

8. Multiple model variants in one manual: profile the requested model only. Ignore
   accessories and requirements that apply only to other variants. If the manual
   does not distinguish, say so in confidence.notes and lower confidence.overall.

9. evidence: 1-8 objects {supports_field, manual_section, note}. supports_field is
   the schema path justified. manual_section is a section title/heading (verbatim
   titles are expected). note is a <=12-word paraphrase — never copy manual
   sentences. Prefer coverage across consequential fields — not multiple evidence
   rows for one field. Fill in this priority order (stop when the cap is reached):
   (a) data_roles fields set true, and each requires_devices entry;
   (b) safety_role fields set true;
   (c) representative operator_actions (at least the emergency-context ones);
   (d) control_surfaces.
   Do not invent extra mandatory evidence beyond what this priority covers.

10. Optional protected_by / protects / supply_requirements: record when the
    manual explicitly states guard devices or supply needs — including in
    installer-framed installation chapters. audience applies to
    operator_actions only, never to requirement fields. Otherwise empty arrays.

11. safety_role keys are exactly: is_protective_device, has_manual_override,
    has_emergency_procedure. Do not invent other keys.

CALIBRATION — these examples concern OTHER devices / situations. Match the
field decisions (context, audience, optional_accessory, dependency shape).
Do NOT copy action strings or product-specific wording from the examples into
the device under extraction unless that wording appears in THIS device's
manual excerpts.

A. "To connect shore power: set the AC input current limit to match the dock
   supply using the rotary selector."
   → context: situational; audience: operator; one verb-phrase action naming

B. "Battery type selection must be performed at commissioning. Consult your
   Balmar dealer for programming assistance."
   → context: commissioning; audience: installer_or_technician; one action for

C. "If the BMS has disconnected the battery, remove the fault cause, then press

D. "The charger can be monitored and configured via the VictronConnect app over
